mnemonics.title: DFD Components Memory
mnemonics.content: Remember DFD symbols using: 'PEDS' - Process = Circle, External Entity = Square, Data Store = Open Rectangle, Data Flow = Arrow (think of arrow as flowing 'S'tream)

mnemonics.title: DFD Levels
mnemonics.content: Context (0) → Level 1 → Level 2... Think: 'Zero is the Hero (Context), then we Level Up!'

mnemonics.title: Process Validation
mnemonics.content: Every process needs INPUT → PROCESS → OUTPUT. Remember: 'No In, No Out, What it's all about!'

acronyms.usage: The primary tool for flow-oriented modeling showing how data moves through a system
The arrow/line showing data movement between DFD components
Source or destination of data outside the system
Transformation that converts input data to output data
Highest level DFD (Level 0) showing system as single process

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a factory: External Entities are suppliers/customers (outside), Processes are machines (transform), Data Stores are warehouses (storage), Data Flows are conveyor belts (movement)
Think of DFD levels like zoom levels in Google Maps: Context = whole city, Level 1 = districts, Level 2 = streets - each level shows more detail
Think of DFD levels as budget sheets: What goes in (inputs) must equal what comes out (outputs) at each level
Logical = 'What' (think: recipe - ingredients and steps), Physical = 'How' (think: actual cooking with specific utensils and stove)

analogies.analogy: Think of DFD like a water pipeline system: External entities are water sources/taps, Processes are filters/treatment plants, Data stores are reservoirs/tanks, Data flows are the pipes carrying water
External entities are senders/receivers, Processes are sorting/delivery departments, Data stores are mailboxes/archives, Data flows are the mail trucks/routes
If the system was a person: DFD shows what they DO (activities, workflows), ERD shows what they KNOW (information, memories, relationships)
